methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.7x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Multiply Labs is a Series A biotech company founded by Fred Parietti and Alice Melocchi that has raised $41M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 11 comparable companies—3 public-market peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies in biotech and health spanning Seed through Series B, including Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Xaira Therapeutics ($2.7B), Neko Health ($1.8B), and Chai Discovery ($1.3B).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis uses the $85M total-raised proxy as a base and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $170M. Peer-Median analysis across the 7 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a $1.3B median valuation, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (6.7x median) cannot be applied without disclosed revenue figures, which private companies typically withhold.

The triangulated range is $144M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $735M. This wide dispersion reflects the heterogeneity of early-stage biotech valuations and reflects two competing reference frames: a conservative stage-based approach anchored to capital raised, versus market-based peer multiples that incorporate significant premiums for frontier-tech positioning. Key uncertainties include target revenue and unit economics, technology maturity and IP strength, and whether peer comparables are truly stage-aligned or inflated by sector-wide froth.
